# UC-22.14.54 · Security Impact Analysis Signals for Emergency Changes

## Description

Flags emergency changes with subsequent security alerts for CM-4 security impact analysis feedback.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST 800-53 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes itsm and sourcetypes snow:change_request. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Require CHG id in ES annotations via workflow; (2) tune join window; (3) monthly emergency change review deck; (4) document false positive handling. Indexes required: `index=itsm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=snow:change_request`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928), Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `snow:change_request` type=emergency, `` `notable` ``.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Require CHG id in ES annotations via workflow; (2) tune join window; (3) monthly emergency change review deck; (4) document false positive handling.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=snow:change_request type=emergency earliest=-30d
| eval cid=number
| join type=left cid [
    search `notable` earliest=-30d
    | rex field=annotations "(?i)chg:(?<cid>CHG[0-9]+)"
    | stats count by cid
  ]
| where count>0
```

Understanding this SPL

**Security Impact Analysis Signals for Emergency Changes (CM-4)** — Flags emergency changes with subsequent security alerts for CM-4 security impact analysis feedback.

Documented **Data sources**: `snow:change_request` type=emergency, `` `notable` ``. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928), Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: snow:change_request. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype=snow:change_request, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **cid** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where count>0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: cid.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare a sample of the same events in the source system (domain controller, cloud tenant, or device) the security team would open first. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (changes with notables), Bar chart (alert types)

## SPL

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=snow:change_request type=emergency earliest=-30d
| eval cid=number
| join type=left cid [
    search `notable` earliest=-30d
    | rex field=annotations "(?i)chg:(?<cid>CHG[0-9]+)"
    | stats count by cid
  ]
| where count>0
```

## Visualization

Table (changes with notables), Bar chart (alert types). Key fields: `cid`.
